# MMM Framework — Bayesian Marketing Mix Modeling & the Augur platform

> MMM Framework is a production-ready, open-source Bayesian Marketing Mix Modeling (MMM) library built on PyMC, and Augur, the application that runs the whole causal measurement loop on top of it. It emphasizes genuine uncertainty quantification, pre-registered analysis to limit researcher degrees of freedom, and calibration of model estimates against real-world experiments (geo lift, matched-market tests).

Marketing Mix Modeling answers a causal question — what *incremental* sales did each marketing channel cause — not merely what moved together. The docs below cover the modeling method (adstock, saturation, hierarchical Bayesian inference), the identification assumptions it rests on, and the Augur platform (the Oracle agent workspace, the Auspices experiment planner, and the Atelier for authoring bespoke model families). Key concepts: adstock/carryover, saturation/diminishing returns, ROI and marginal ROAS, posterior credible intervals, experiment calibration, and the EIG/EVOI experiment-prioritization loop.

## Start here
- [Honest Causal Marketing Mix Modeling](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/index.html): An open-source Bayesian MMM built for honest causal measurement: pre-registered model designs, methodology pressure-tested in public against known ground truth, verified uncertainty calibration, and estimates calibrated against real experiments.
- [Getting Started](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/getting-started.html): Install the MMM Framework, fit your first Bayesian marketing mix model, and walk through a complete code example end to end.
- [Augur](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/platform-overview.html): A guided tour of Augur, the MMM Framework platform: Orrery (program), Auspices (experiments), Sextant (continuous learning), Chronicle (performance), Almanac (budget planner), Constellation (portfolio), the Oracle agent workspace, the Atelier for authoring bespoke models, Codex knowledge base, and the College & Sanctum — one place to run the whole causal measurement loop.
- [FAQ](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/faq.html): Frequently asked questions about the MMM Framework — answers to common objections and practical questions about Bayesian Marketing Mix Modeling, from data requirements to interpreting results.
- [Troubleshooting](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/troubleshooting.html): A troubleshooting runbook for the MMM Framework — fixes for install failures (JAX on Apple Silicon, PyTensor/clang on macOS), slow or diverging samplers, MFF data-validation errors, model load/serialization across versions, and web-app/Redis issues.
- [Glossary](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/glossary.html): Definitions of the key terms used across the MMM Framework documentation - adstock, saturation, EIG, EVOI, MFF, ITT, MDE, ROPE, and more.

## Learn the framework
- [Modeling Guide](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/modeling-guide.html): Step-by-step guide for implementing statistically sound marketing mix models using the MMM Framework.
- [Real-Data Onboarding](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/real-data-guide.html): The real-data path: a deliberately messy client CSV taken through cleaning, MFF assembly, the EDA gate, fitting, and the validation battery that substitutes for ground truth when none exists — every number measured from the baked notebook.
- [Interpreting Results](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/interpreting-results.html): A guide for media planners and CMOs on interpreting marketing mix model results, understanding uncertainty, and making confident budget decisions.
- [Reading the Report](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/reading-the-report.html): A section-by-section guide to reading an MMM Framework report — what the model-fit, ROI forest, decomposition waterfall, saturation and adstock curves, prior-vs-posterior, posterior-predictive checks, and estimand credible intervals each show, and the budget decision each one supports.
- [Data-Prep Cookbook](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/data-prep-cookbook.html): A code cookbook for turning raw ad-platform exports (Google Ads, Meta, GA4) into a Master Flat File (MFF) ready for the MMM Framework — joining spend and impressions, aligning weekly cadence, handling missing periods, and choosing spend vs impressions.
- [Migration Guide](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/migration-guide.html): A migration guide for moving an MMM from Robyn, Google Meridian, or PyMC-Marketing to the MMM Framework — how hyperparameter ranges become priors, how adstock/saturation and geo hierarchy map across, and where experiment calibration and pre-registration differ.
- [For Business Stakeholders](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/business-stakeholders.html): Understanding the risks of specification shopping in marketing mix modeling for ad agencies, model shops, and brands.
- [Data Requirements & Runtime](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/data-requirements.html): What data the MMM Framework needs and what it costs to run: the MFF input format, minimum history and variation guidance, and measured fit times on commodity hardware.

## Methodology & causal foundations
- [Causal Inference Guide](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-inference.html): How Marketing Mix Modeling estimates incremental causal effects: potential outcomes, structural causal models, confounding and back-door adjustment, and why correlation is not ROI.
- [Bayesian Workflow](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/bayesian-workflow.html): The principled Bayesian workflow for marketing mix models: choosing priors, prior and posterior predictive checks, sampling diagnostics, and iterative model criticism.
- [Scientific Statistical Modeling](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/scientific-modeling.html): How rigorous statistical modeling underpins the MMM Framework: generative models, identifiability, pre-specified analyses, and the Bayesian workflow.
- [Variable Selection](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/variable-selection.html): Choosing what an MMM controls for by causal structure — telling confounders, mediators, and colliders apart — instead of stepwise selection or specification shopping.
- [Measurement & Calibration](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/measurement-calibration.html): A comprehensive guide to closed-loop measurement: how to integrate experiments and Marketing Mix Models into a single, self-correcting calibration cycle that compounds learning and improves budget decisions.
- [Continuous Learning](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/continuous-learning.html): For media analysts: how continuous sequential learning plans a budget from designed experiments — which channels work, whether they help or hurt each other, how much to move, and when to stop testing — with the uncertainty attached.
- [Continuous Learning · Mathematical Foundations](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/continuous-learning-math.html): A rigorous, code-faithful derivation of the continuous-learning loop: expected information gain (Lindley), the Gaussian/Laplace EIG surrogate, D- and Ds-optimality, the knowledge gradient (EVSI), Thompson allocation, expected regret, and the ENBS stopping rule.
- [Identification Assumptions](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/identification-assumptions.html): The identification contract: the seven assumptions every causal claim in this framework rests on — stated formally, labeled testable or untestable, priced against the pressure-testing scorecard, and matched to the framework feature that addresses each one.
- [Technical Guide](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/technical-guide.html): Technical reference for the MMM Framework: model specifications, adstock and saturation parameterizations, Bayesian inference settings, and the extended model families.

## Platform (Augur)
- [Augur](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/platform-overview.html): A guided tour of Augur, the MMM Framework platform: Orrery (program), Auspices (experiments), Sextant (continuous learning), Chronicle (performance), Almanac (budget planner), Constellation (portfolio), the Oracle agent workspace, the Atelier for authoring bespoke models, Codex knowledge base, and the College & Sanctum — one place to run the whole causal measurement loop.
- [Model Garden & Atelier](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/model-garden.html): The Model Garden turns a methodologist's bespoke Bayesian model into a named, versioned, org-shared asset. Author it once in the Atelier, prove it against the compatibility suite, publish it — and every analyst fits, interrogates, and reports on it through the agent without touching the kernel.
- [Pricing](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/pricing.html): Open-core pricing for the MMM Framework: a free, self-hosted OSS core with no limits, plus paid managed tiers (Free / Team / Business / Enterprise) whose seat, project, and monthly-fit limits and feature gates are exactly the ones enforced in code. Dollar prices are proposed and illustrative; billing is per-seat plus metered fits.
- [Data Connections](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/data-connections.html): How to bring data into Augur: upload a file, or save a live connection to BigQuery or Google Cloud Storage that syncs on demand or on a schedule. Authentication, sync model, security, and the recommended path for ad-platform spend.
- [Trust & Security](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/trust.html): Buyer-facing trust & security overview for the MMM Framework platform: multi-tenant isolation, IDOR-safe 404s, code-execution sandboxing, secret hygiene & egress controls, SSRF protection, tamper-evident audit logging, data handling, encryption posture, and authentication (JWT now, SSO/OIDC-ready). Every control is tied to source.
- [Security & Deployment Posture](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/security.html): InfoSec reference for the MMM Framework platform: which LLM providers see what data, code-execution sandboxing tiers, the hosted multi-user profile, authentication status, audit logging, data residency, and a full outbound-network inventory.
- [Responsible Disclosure](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/responsible-disclosure.html): How to report a security vulnerability in the MMM Framework: contact channels, scope, what to expect, safe-harbor guidance, and the machine-readable security.txt. This is self-hosted, open-source software — there is no vendor-operated SaaS.
- [Evaluator's Guide](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/evaluator.html): Procurement facts on one page: license, version, maintainership, API stability, support model, measured evidence, and known limitations of the MMM Framework.

## Proof, demos & worked examples
- [Demos & Reports](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/demos.html): Interactive workflow demonstrations, example reports, and scenario analyses for the MMM Framework.
- [The MMM Walkthrough](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/mmm-walkthrough.html): The full analyst workflow on a realistic — but not adversarial — synthetic world: EDA pre-flight, a pre-specified model, the naive-fit failure, the causal-structure recovery, experiment calibration, and decision outputs, every step graded against known truth.
- [Marketing Mix Model Report](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/mmm-example-report.html): Sample MMM report from a Q4 2025 fit: posterior summaries, channel decompositions, ROI estimates, and budget recommendations.
- [Causal Features Showcase](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-features-showcase.html): A guided tour of the MMM Framework's causal feature stack: DAG role typing, identification checks, confounding sensitivity, experiment-calibrated priors and likelihoods, the refutation suite, and causal-assumptions reporting — with measured outputs and honest limits.
- [Pressure Testing](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/pressure-testing.html): How the MMM Framework is pressure-tested: 16 synthetic worlds with known causal ground truth, the measured silent-failure scorecard, and the framework's known strengths and weaknesses.
- [Interactive MMM Workflow](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/scientific-workflow-demo.html): An interactive nine-step walkthrough of the full scientific MMM workflow, from data preparation through diagnostics, decomposition, and reporting.
- [Scientific Modeling for Everyone](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/scientific-workflow-simple.html): An accessible introduction to the scientific MMM workflow for analysts new to Bayesian modeling - the same steps as the full workflow, with less math.
- [Business Readiness Report](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/business-readiness-report.html): Business readiness assessment and commercialization roadmap (P0–P2) for the MMM Framework — a causal, agent-operated Bayesian marketing measurement platform.

## Task workflows
- [Budget Optimization](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/workflow-budget-optimization.html): Use a calibrated MMM to allocate budget across channels, run sensitivity analyses, and compare scenarios under uncertainty.
- [Channel Effectiveness](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/workflow-channel-effectiveness.html): Quantify channel-level ROI, marginal returns, and saturation curves from the MMM posterior - with credible intervals you can defend.
- [Forecasting](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/workflow-forecasting.html): Generate marketing-driven outcome forecasts with credible intervals using the MMM Framework - scenario projections that quantify what we don't know.
- [Calibration & Decision Theory Workshop](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/workflow-calibration-decisions.html): An interactive, decision-theoretic workshop on calibrating MMMs through experimentation - EIG, EVOI, constraint-aware portfolio selection, and the multi-cycle calibration loop, end to end.

## Project
- [About](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/about.html): Background on the MMM Framework, its design principles around methodological rigor and uncertainty quantification, and the audiences it is built for.
- [Versioning & Changelog](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/changelog.html): Current version, API stability status, and release history for the MMM Framework.

## Optional

These are deep, multi-part series and downloadable templates — read on demand; skip if context is limited.

### Mathematics series
- [Math 00: The Generative Model](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/math-00-overview.html): The MMM as a generative model: the full additive linear predictor, the Normal likelihood, every default prior, the spend-to-contribution pipeline, and a live prior-predictive simulation you can re-draw in the browser.
- [Math 01: Adstock](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/math-01-adstock.html): The mathematics of adstock (carryover): the geometric recurrence and its 1/(1−α) multiplier, normalized FIR kernels, the geometric/delayed/Weibull families in an interactive explorer, the parametric default vs the legacy Beta(2,2) blend-weight mechanism, and what l_max truncation cuts off.
- [Math 02: Saturation](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/math-02-saturation.html): The mathematics of diminishing returns in the MMM Framework: the core exponential curve 1 − e^(−λx) and its half-saturation point ln2/λ, why it is strictly concave (not S-shaped), the Hill alternative with genuine thresholds, normalization, and the β·f contribution ceiling — with a live curve explorer.
- [Math 03: Seasonality & Trend](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/math-03-seasonality-trend.html): The mathematics of the MMM baseline: Fourier seasonality as a bias-variance dial, and three trend families — linear, piecewise/Prophet-style slope changes, and B-spline partition of unity — with interactive derivations.
- [Math 04: The Bayesian Model](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/math-04-bayesian-model.html): The full Bayesian MMM pipeline run end-to-end on the Aurora data: priors times likelihood, the prior predictive, NUTS in plain math, convergence diagnostics, posterior intervals, the posterior-predictive check, and the additive component decomposition — with the measured numbers from the baked notebook.
- [Math 05: Calibration](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/math-05-calibration.html): Identifiability and experiment calibration in the MMM Framework: the equifinality trap, the design-factor algebra that turns a lift test into a prior, the in-graph likelihood route, and the measured Aurora demo where calibration pulls Search ROAS from 1.04 toward its true 0.66.
- [Math 06: Extensions](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/math-06-extensions.html): The mathematics of the MMM Framework's extension models: NestedMMM mediation (direct vs indirect effects, proportion mediated), MultivariateMMM cross-effects (the psi matrix vs correlated residuals), and CombinedMMM as their routed synthesis — with the measured Aurora results.

### Pressure-testing / stress series
- [Stress Test 00: The Rosy Picture](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/stress-00-rosy-picture.html): The doctrine-setting opener of the stress series: a clean positive control recovers at 7% median error — and an equally green demand-confounded fit badly over-credits Search. What each diagnostic can and cannot see.
- [Stress Test 01: Carryover & Shape](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/stress-01-carryover-shape.html): Delayed-Weibull carryover vs a geometric kernel (median error 88%), Hill thresholds vs a concave default (+38% total media, all diagnostics green), and 15× spend spikes (coverage 0%) — three functional-form failures and the measured pivots that repair them.
- [Stress Test 02: Time Structure](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/stress-02-time-structure.html): A trend break the brand reacted to sends TV to −63% under the default linear trend; evolving seasonality scrambles the channel split while totals stay right; effectiveness drift hides inside green totals. The measured failures and the pivots that fix them.
- [Stress Test 03: Confounding & Selection](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/stress-03-confounding-selection.html): The causal core of the stress series: demand-chasing spend inflates a channel +110% with every diagnostic green, a noisy proxy control leaves +35% residual bias, near-perfect collinearity makes the split a posterior seesaw, and one lift test does what no prior can.
- [Stress Test 04: Extension Traps](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/stress-04-extension-traps.html): When 'more model' cures and when it just adds knobs: the base MMM loses a mediated channel, NestedMMM surfaces the hidden mediation but only partially recovers it (the mediated channels stay weakly identified, TV's total-effect interval spanning zero), a cross-effect relocates to a sizeable negative and flips sign with the prior, and a wrong mediator fits perfectly.
- [Stress Test 05: The Gauntlet](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/stress-05-gauntlet.html): Everything broken at once: the Aurora kitchen-sink world inverts the channel ranking and overstates Search 8x. The full recovery workflow — EDA, triage, structural pivots, experiment calibration — each step measured against known truth.
- [Stress Test 06: Geography & Hierarchy](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/stress-06-geo-hierarchy.html): Panel worlds with per-geography ground truth: the cross-geo adstock-bleed bug this notebook found and fixed, a national-green model whose per-geo errors hit +330%, TV's regional ROI ranking fully inverted (Spearman rho = -1), and the split-fit pivot that repairs the regional readout.

### Bayesian workshop series (beginner)
- [Workshop 00: Thinking in Distributions](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/workshop-00-thinking-in-distributions.html): A from-zero introduction to Bayesian thinking for marketing analysts: probability as belief, Bayes' rule on a grid, priors, credible intervals and HDIs, and Monte Carlo — taught through one A/B test, with every computation running live in your browser.
- [Workshop 01: Priors](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/workshop-01-priors.html): Workshop 01 of the Bayesian MMM workshop: flat vs weakly-informative vs informative priors, the MMM distribution zoo (Beta, Gamma, HalfNormal), priors on parameters as priors on behavior, prior predictive checks, and the framework's PriorConfig API.
- [Workshop 02: Sampling](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/workshop-02-sampling.html): How MCMC actually works, for analysts: why grids die, Monte Carlo, a 20-line Metropolis sampler you can drive (and break) in your browser, R-hat and ESS computed live on your own chains, NUTS and divergences in plain English, and a trace-plot reading clinic.
- [Workshop 03: Your First MMM](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/workshop-03-first-mmm.html): Fit your first Bayesian marketing mix model end to end: the MMM vocabulary, the panel data, ModelConfig choice by choice, the diagnostics gate, and a first fit graded against a known answer key — ~7% median error with truth inside every 90% HDI.
- [Workshop 04: Reading the Posterior](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/workshop-04-reading-the-posterior.html): Workshop 04 of the Bayesian MMM workshop series: the posterior as a table of internally-consistent worlds — HDI vs equal-tailed intervals, forest plots, the beta–lambda trade-off (r ≈ −0.8) that forces per-draw computation, prior→posterior contraction, posterior-predictive checks, and decomposition with honest bands.
- [Workshop 05: From Draws to Decisions](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/workshop-05-from-draws-to-decisions.html): The capstone of the Bayesian MMM workshop: compute per draw, summarize last. ROAS as a distribution, decision probabilities, marginal vs average ROAS, response curves with bands, and a budget reallocation graded against known truth.

### Aurora framework tour
- [Aurora 00: The Trap](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/aurora-00-overview.html): Aurora Coffee Co. has to decide next quarter's budget. The dashboard crowns Search (correlation 0.85) — but the sealed answer key says Search loses money (true ROAS 0.66) while TV is the best investment the brand has. The hook of the six-part Aurora showcase.
- [Aurora 01: Causality](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/aurora-01-causality.html): Chapter 1 of the Aurora Coffee Co. showcase: the causal DAG behind the data, back-door identification, automatic bad-control detection, and why a geo-lift experiment — not more controls — is what finally pulls Search ROAS from 2.51 down to its true 0.66.
- [Aurora 02: The Base MMM](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/aurora-02-base-mmm.html): Chapter 2 of the Aurora Coffee Co. showcase: fitting the core Bayesian MMM, gating on diagnostics, reading contributions and ROAS with credible intervals, marginal ROAS, what-if scenarios — and the honest residual the base model cannot see.
- [Aurora 03: Extended Models](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/aurora-03-extended-mmm.html): What is TV really doing? NestedMMM shows TV and Display work through brand awareness, but on PyMC 6 only partially recovers the mediated share (proportion mediated ~0.69 TV / ~0.40 Display vs a true ~0.99/0.97); MultivariateMMM puts the Cold Brew cannibalization claim through an honesty test — and finds the cross-effect weakly identified.
- [Aurora 04: Reporting](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/aurora-04-reporting.html): Aurora chapter 4: turning a fitted Bayesian MMM into a board-ready HTML report — MMMReportGenerator, themed design tokens, ROI with credible intervals, and the uncertainty-communication doctrine for executives.
- [Aurora 05: The Unified Workflow](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/aurora-05-unified-workflow.html): The Aurora Coffee Co. finale: one causal pipeline — data, DAG, fit, experiment calibration, extensions — ending in a budget reallocation worth ≈ $11.9M/yr more than the dashboard's plan on the same budget.

### Causal inference series
- [Causal Inference 00: The Ladder of Evidence](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-00-the-ladder.html): Naive attribution inflates Social 1.8× and reads the best channel lowest; even a full Bayesian MMM over-credits Search +43%. Rung 0 of the causal ladder.
- [Causal Inference 01: Confounding & Adjustment](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-01-confounding-adjustment.html): A demand-index control cuts Search bias +43%→+25% and Social +38%→+18% — half the door. Noisy proxies leave the rest; budget pacing has no door to close.
- [Causal Inference 02: The MMM as a Causal Model](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-02-mmm-as-causal-model.html): Graded against truth: 5/7 channels within ±20%, a mediator control amputates TV/Video 18–23%, refutation passes 3/4 yet misses TV's −18% bias (0.996 ridge).
- [Causal Inference 03: Structural Mediation](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-03-structural-mediation.html): Model the funnel instead of conditioning on it: a structural MMM recovers all 5 mechanism parameters, three latent series (demand corr 0.95), and each channel's mediated share — graded against a sealed answer key.
- [Causal Inference 04: Latent Confounders](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-04-latent-confounders.html): A latent economic confounder over-credits Search +778%. Indicators-as-controls cut it to +172%; a joint latent-factor MMM ties on points and recovers the measurement model itself (factor corr 0.98).
- [Causal Inference 05: Measuring One Experiment](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-05-measuring-one-experiment.html): 26% false positives from the 'sophisticated' estimator on null data, ~5% after placebo calibration, 100% power at the expected effect — pre-registering a 10-market geo lift test.
- [Causal Inference 06: Calibrating the Model](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-06-calibrating-the-model.html): A 0.49 ± 0.08 lift readout meets a +43% confounded Search: the prior route leaves +42%, the in-graph likelihood lands +5% — and the wrong estimand silently voids the test (+38%).
- [Causal Inference 07: Many Experiments](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-07-many-experiments.html): Radio −36%→−10% with interval width −74% from one go-dark, Display width −54% from an off-panel holdout, and a z=2.8 legacy study that drags Search +8%→+26% — evidence as a portfolio.
- [Causal Inference 08: Designing the Next Experiment](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-08-designing-next-experiment.html): The default TV flighting test buys an 'inconclusive' — MDE 1.46 vs an expected readout of 0.39. The optimizer's ±21% × 16-week design fixes it; every national Print test is a no.
- [Causal Inference 09: The Measurement Program](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-09-measurement-program.html): Print tops the EIG × EVOI grid (1.17 nats, 634 KPI units); Radio is retest-due 83 weeks on; EVPI 1,624 ≈ 4.4% of budget value caps the measurement program.
- [Causal Inference 10: The Closed Loop](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/causal-10-closed-loop.html): Three fit → design → measure → calibrate cycles on a confounded world: attribution error 31.5% → 17.6%, EVPI 350 → 237, and a −923 KPI round-0 budget plan repaired by +788.

### Modern measurement research (blog)
- [Modern Measurement Research](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog.html): Research essays on modern marketing measurement: causal inference, geo experiments, Bayesian MMM, experimental design, bandits, and the pitfalls in between.
- [Activity Bias: Why Observational Ad Measurement Flatters Itself](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-activity-bias.html): Activity bias in ad measurement: why observational exposed-vs-unexposed comparisons overstate ad effects by 100x or more, and why controls can't fix it.
- [Uncovering Causal Estimates from Non-Experimental Data](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-causal-estimates-observational.html): Causal inference from observational data: a decision guide to adjustment, DAGs, instrumental variables, DiD, regression discontinuity, and synthetic control.
- [Geo Experiments: TBR, Power, and the Stationarity You're Assuming](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-geo-experiments-tbr.html): Classical geo-experiment methodology: GBR design and power analysis, the TBR counterfactual estimator, and the stationarity assumption that carries it.
- [Synthetic Control, Done Right](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-synthetic-control.html): Synthetic control for marketing incrementality: the convex-weights estimator, when it applies, the bias bound, placebo inference, and modern extensions.
- [Modern Staggered Difference-in-Differences](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-staggered-did.html): Why two-way fixed effects breaks under staggered rollouts, and the Callaway–Sant'Anna rebuild: group-time ATT(g,t), doubly robust estimation, uniform inference.
- [CausalImpact: Counterfactuals from Bayesian Structural Time Series](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-causalimpact-bsts.html): CausalImpact estimates campaign lift by forecasting the counterfactual: BSTS state-space models, spike-and-slab control selection, honest uncertainty.
- [Carryover and Shape Effects in Bayesian MMM](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-bayesian-mmm-carryover-shape.html): Geometric vs delayed adstock, Hill saturation, weak identification, and why average ROAS is not marginal ROAS — the Jin et al. (2017) Bayesian MMM.
- [Adstock Dynamics and the Timing of Sequential Experiments](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-carryover-experiment-timing.html): Adstock carryover contaminates experiments: how to size measurement windows, set washout periods from the retention rate, and space sequential media tests.
- [Common Pitfalls in Statistical Modeling](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-modeling-pitfalls.html): Statistical modeling pitfalls: a field guide to confounding, forking paths, overfitting, missing data, and Type S/M errors — and the remedies that work.
- [Hundreds of Models, One Winner: Multiple Comparisons and the Winner's Curse](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-multiple-comparisons.html): Select the best of hundreds of model runs and its performance is exaggerated: Type S/M errors, the winner's curse, and why partial pooling beats Bonferroni.
- [From Lindley to Deep Adaptive Design: 65 Years of Bayesian Experimental Design](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-lindley-to-dad.html): Bayesian experimental design from Lindley's 1956 information measure to Deep Adaptive Design: EIG, nested Monte Carlo, variational bounds, policy networks.
- [A Geo-Holdout Is a Bayesian Experimental Design: Computing Its EIG](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-geo-holdout-eig.html): Encode a geo-holdout as a design vector, use the MMM posterior as the prior, and the value of the test becomes computable: expected information gain.
- [BED vs. Bayesian Optimization vs. Bandits for Media Experimentation](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-bed-bo-bandits.html): Bayesian experimental design, Bayesian optimization, and bandits for media experiments: one Bayesian surrogate, three objectives — learn, optimize, or earn.
- [Thompson Sampling: A Practical Tour](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-thompson-sampling.html): Thompson sampling in practice: sample from the posterior, act as if it were true. Beta-Bernoulli mechanics, regret bounds, and media failure modes.
- [Continuous Learning in Media Measurement (with Interaction Effects)](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/blog-continuous-learning-interactions.html): Continuous learning for media measurement: a closed loop of designed experiments, shrinkage priors, and information gain that learns channel interactions.

### Consultant artifacts (templates)
- [Consultant Artifacts](https://redam94.github.io/mmm-framework/artifacts/index.html): Downloadable consultant artifacts for marketing mix modeling engagements — data onboarding and diagnostic checklists, an experiment pre-registration memo, and an executive summary template.
